goal: Add a write executor mount lock that composes activation-lock evidence before any later write execution activation, without writing files, GitHub, database, durable stores, issuing permits, calling providers, executing runtime, merging, or deploying.
scope_out: Do not write files, GitHub, database, durable stores, issue permits, call providers, execute runtime, merge, deploy, change packages, change workflows, or alter retired sandbox write routes.
claims: Bluepilot can preflight write executor mount readiness from Activation-Lock evidence.
The lock requires confirmation, operator execution ref, write mount ref, permit ref, target repo/path refs, content hash ref, and activation_lock_ready for write_action.
Even when executorMountReady is true, writesAllowed remains false.
assumptions: Actual write execution remains a later explicit env-gated operation.
The existing retired sandbox write routes remain unchanged.
dod: GET /probe/write-executor-mount-lock-contract returns the closed mount boundary.
POST /probe/write-executor-mount-lock-preflight validates activation-lock evidence without writes.
Focused tests, typecheck, task-lock verification, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
required_commands: npx tsx --test tests/writeExecutorMountLock.test.ts tests/writeExecutorMountLockRoute.test.ts tests/mayaCoreGateEnforcementContract.test.ts tests/activationLockBoundary.test.ts tests/activationLockRoute.test.ts tests/meta.test.ts
stop_conditions: File write, GitHub write, DB write, durable persistence, permit issue, provider call, runtime execution, merge, deploy, package change, workflow change, or sandbox write reopening becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: Provider Call Executor Mount Lock is merged and live. Write actions now need the equivalent executor mount lock before any write execution activation can be reviewed.
